title: galarhoeus villosus
intro: Galarhoeus villosus, also known as Chinese milk vetch or hairy milk vetch, is a perennial plant native to East Asia, characterized by its hairy stems and leaves. The plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Galarhoeus villosus include reducing fever, alleviating coughs and respiratory issues, and protecting against cardiovascular disease. Traditionally, the plant is prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and it may also be used in combination with other herbs in traditional remedies.
